title: Digital Connection vs. Mental Well-being
content: Introduction to the exploration of the relationship between digital connectivity and its impact on mental health.

title: The Ubiquitous Landscape
content: Gen Z are identified as Digital Natives, experiencing life online. A high percentage of students use platforms like YouTube, Instagram, and Snapchat daily.

title: Usage and Anxiety Statistics
content: Digital usage over 2 hours a day is linked to increased feelings of anxiety and sadness. There is a notable rise in depression risk among heavy users.

title: Psychological Manifestations
content: Digital interaction can lead to issues like FOMO, self-comparison, and cyberbullying. These factors contribute to stress and low self-esteem.
key_claims: Curated feeds lead to negative self-image

title: Strategic Mitigation Efforts
content: Strategies include digital detox and awareness education. Companies must prioritize user well-being as a fundamental responsibility.
key_claims: Digital Detox is crucial for balance
